hints: Call print() once for each line. Each call goes on its own line in your code.
The text you want to print goes inside the parentheses, wrapped in quotes: print("Hello, world!")
Watch out for the apostrophe in "Let's go!" — if you wrap the whole string in single quotes, the apostrophe will break it. Use double quotes on the outside: print("Let's go!")
A comment starts with # and goes on the line above the code it describes: # Print the greeting
